crisis_p_24: Marriages without intimacy are often dead. Divorce becomes a matter of time.

crisis_blockquote_damage_quote_30: “I counted—the shortest time was 10 seconds. I saw her look, and I wished I could disappear into a hole. Now I refuse all intimacy. At least I won't embarrass myself.”
crisis_p_32: From anticipation to horror — the reaction becomes Conditioned. Every time you perpetuate failure.
crisis_strong_33: The silent disappointment of the wife
crisis_p_34: "Don't you find me attractive anymore?" “Are you cheating on me?” - Questions that kill a relationship from within.
crisis_p_36: Room failure spreads like a cancer to work and social life.
crisis_p_38: At three in the morning, she writes “Treatment for Premature Ejaculation” and reads strangers’ stories.
crisis_h3_39: Size anxiety: from the dressing room to the shadow of a lifetime
crisis_blockquote_damage_quote_40: “One comment in the locker room and I was 15 years old, and since that day I have not changed in front of anyone. Watching pornography is not fun — it is torture. I feel like I was born with a defect.”
crisis_p_42: That teenage embarrassment burns the soul and leaves an indelible stain.
crisis_p_44: She hates the mirror. You hate your body. Avoid any situation that shows your private parts.
crisis_strong_45: A scam that targets your weakness
crisis_p_46: Thousands of dollars on fake pills and pumps that hurt you more.
crisis_p_48: “Who would want someone like me?” - So you withdraw before you are rejected.
crisis_h2_50: What these guys go through every day
crisis_p_section_subtitle_51: These voices are from midnight forums, anonymous confessions, and from every soul suffering silently
crisis_p_54: You hide in the bathroom, research “erectile dysfunction treatment,” delete the history, and cry alone. Nobody knows. No one will know.
crisis_p_57: Sighing in disappointment, she sleeps back to back. You're guilty because you can't. She is guilty for expecting. And the wall is growing.
crisis_p_60: Fake laughter, vague insinuations, early departures. You're the best at acting — because you've been acting as a "normal guy" for years.
crisis_p_63: Avoid looking in the mirror. You compare yourself to what you see on screens. You give up. “This is how I was born, and this is how I will die.”
crisis_p_66: You invented a betrayal that you did not commit. I left everything. I walked away with nothing. Because shame is easier than pity. At least hatred carries dignity.
crisis_p_69: $599 on fake pills. $1299 on your wound pump. $2499 for allergic reaction. You borrow from your brother. You lie to your wife.

crisis_div_chats-grid_1: Youssef Al-Rasheed is online now 3:17 My friend... I found your page at 3 in the morning. I can't sleep. I have been married for 10 years, and I have three children. It's been a year since I can't. I stay in the office until midnight. I pray Fajr alone in the mosque. I spent money on everything — rulers, supplements, even Ruqyah Sheikh. Nothing worked.🎤Voice message 0:42 Omar Al Farsi writes... 11:48 It's too early to cancel my engagement. Because I'll finish before we start. Every time. We have been together for 8 months. She told her cousin that she does not feel that she is a real wife. ⌫ Deleted message Khaled Al-Hassan is online now 2:20 Sami arranged an engagement for me. The wedding is in two months. One comment. In the dressing room, I am 16 years old. Since that day, I have not showered in front of anyone. I would rather be alone than embarrass two families. Samir Al-Masry is online now 1:05 The wedding salila failed. It's been 3 months and I'm a failure. She covers her face and cries quietly. Her brothers ask why she's not pregnant. Shame kills my parents.🎤Voice message 1:15 Tariq Al-Omar Last appearance 5 min ago 12:33 I told my wife that I got married again. A lie. We spent 6 years and everything on treatments. Finally God blessed us with a son. Sometimes I think about ending everything. Then I hear him laughing. I can't. Let her hate me. Hatred is easier than pity. At least there is dignity in hate. A person like you is online now. If you are reading this now...your dignity is not measured in one night. Your value as a man is not measured in minutes. Do not give up. not yet. Not today.
